canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Proactivity is predominantly characterized as a conversational agent's tendency to take initiative rather than being purely reactive. The most common definition describes it as an agent's ability to guide a user towards a goal, with some papers noting this involves planning ahead and guiding the conversation flow. A more specific framing defines it as the tendency to initiate topic shifts or provide unsolicited information, sometimes in the context of a "proactive transition between task and chitchat". A distinct line of work, using the term "Initiating collaboration," conceptualizes it as an agent's ability to recognize its own limitations and request assistance from other agents. The construct is operationalized in several ways; one paper reports measuring it through the accuracy of "proactive actions (Acc P)", while another source indicates that a Reward model is used for measurement. Proactivity is also studied in relation to Collaboration, with one paper noting that models are often better at "responding to collaboration than initiating collaboration."
papers: Knowledge Image Matters: Improving Knowledge-Based Visual Reasoning with Multi-Image Large Language Models
Understanding Large Language Model Vulnerabilities to Social Bias Attacks
CARE: A Disagreement Detection Framework with Concept Alignment and Reasoning Enhancement
Model-based Large Language Model Customization as Service
When Life Gives You Samples: The Benefits of Scaling up Inference Compute for MultilingualLLMs

definitions.text: The agent's tendency to take initiative in a dialogue to guide the user towards a goal, rather than only passively responding.
definitions.paper: Understanding Large Language Model Vulnerabilities to Social Bias Attacks

definitions.text: The latent tendency of a conversational agent to initiate topic shifts or provide unsolicited but relevant information without explicit user prompting.
The ability of an agent to recognize its own limitations based on the task and environment, and to generate a request for assistance from other agents.
definitions.paper: Model-based Large Language Model Customization as Service

relationships.paper: Proactive Agent: Shifting LLM Agents from Reactive Responses to Active Assistance

iclr25: Proactive Agent: Shifting LLM Agents from Reactive Responses to Active Assistance

relationships.paper: Model-based Large Language Model Customization as Service
